+{"id":"bd-dkms6","title":"[HIGH] build_btrfs_image fixture no longer stamps root-tree tree_block_csum","description":"# Review finding

`build_btrfs_image()` is still constructing a synthetic btrfs root-tree leaf without stamping its `tree_block_csum` field.

## Evidence
- Builder: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22119-22204`
- It writes a leaf header and item payload at `root_logical = 0x4000`, but never calls the existing test CRC helper used elsewhere (`stamp_btrfs_test_tree_block_crc32c` at `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22283-22287`).
- The workspace test rerun still fails all `build_btrfs_image()` consumers with `Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`:
  - `open_fs_from_btrfs_image`
  - `open_fs_btrfs_debug_format`
  - `open_fs_btrfs_superblock_accessor`
  - `open_fs_btrfs_walk_root_tree`

## Why this is high severity
This builder underpins the basic btrfs open/root-tree smoke tests. Right now those tests are red because the fixture no longer satisfies the parser’s checksum contract.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

## Expected fix direction
Stamp the synthetic root-tree block checksum after finalizing the leaf contents, and confirm the minimal open/root-tree tests go green independently of the larger readdir fixture family.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:04:27.513003227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:04:27.513003227Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","fixtures","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}
 {"id":"bd-dm5m","title":"Add ext4 inline-data adversarial fuzz regression coverage","description":"Fallback /testing-fuzzing round: add deterministic adversarial coverage for ext4 inline-data inode/xattr-body parser boundaries so fuzz-relevant malformed seeds are regression-tested without requiring a live fuzz campaign.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldCitadel","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:46:39.650249983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T02:54:48.367744967Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T02:54:48.365563188Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 inline-data adversarial fuzz regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ext4","fuzz"]}
 {"id":"bd-dn1hf","title":"[HIGH] ffs-core btrfs root-tree and readdir tests now fail on CRC32C checksum mismatch","description":"# Review finding

Workspace gate `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` currently fails 10 `ffs-core` tests that all unwrap the same parser error: `Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`.

## Failed tests
- `tests::open_fs_from_btrfs_image`
- `tests::open_fs_btrfs_debug_format`
- `tests::open_fs_btrfs_superblock_accessor`
- `tests::open_fs_btrfs_walk_root_tree`
- `tests::walk_btrfs_dir_entry_items_includes_dir_index_items`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_mixed_types`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_empty_directory_returns_dot_dotdot`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_sorted_by_index`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_offset_pagination`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_special_characters_in_names`

## Failure sites
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22693`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22711`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22723`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22736`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23342`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23365`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23388`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23409`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23442`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23489`

## Why this is high severity
This is a workspace-red regression in core synthetic btrfs coverage. It breaks basic `OpenFs::from_device()` and `readdir()` expectations for internal fixtures, so new btrfs behavior cannot be trusted until the checksum stamping / parser-validation mismatch is resolved.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

## Expected fix direction
Audit recent btrfs fixture builders or validation-path changes so synthetic root-tree and dir-index blocks have checksums stamped consistently with current parser verification.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T07:47:51.099879377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T07:47:51.099879377Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}

 {"id":"bd-otox","title":"EXT4_INLINE_DATA_FL write rejection breaks e2compr conformance test","description":"Commit 7fb649e added a blanket rejection of writes to inodes with EXT4_INLINE_DATA_FL.  {"id":"bd-owq","title":"ffs-error: Define canonical error taxonomy + mapping boundaries","description":"Goal: make error handling consistent across layers:
- ffs-ondisk returns ParseError (pure parsing)
- ffs-core/ffs-fuse/ffs-cli return FfsError (user-facing)

Deliverables:
- Document the mapping from ParseError -> FfsError (which variants, and when we preserve details).
- Ensure ffs-error stays independent of ffs-ondisk (avoid cyclic deps).
- Update docs to reference the canonical listing in crates/ffs-error/src/lib.rs.

Acceptance:
- No doc section lists a different FfsError variant set than the code.
- FUSE errno mapping is complete and matches chosen policy.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:37.381664627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T08:51:43.466728917Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T08:51:43.466701065Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation"]}
+{"id":"bd-oyq4b","title":"[HIGH] ffs-cli btrfs mount test helper exceeds clippy too_many_lines","description":"# Review finding

The fresh workspace clippy rerun exposed an additional red-gate error in `ffs-cli` test code:

## Failure
- File: `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs:5898`
- Item: `fn write_test_btrfs_regular_file_tree(...)`
- Lint: `clippy::too_many_lines`
- Current size: 108 lines vs 100-line limit

## Why this matters
This is now part of the enforced workspace clippy failure set under `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`. Even after fixing the `ffs-tui` lifetime issue, this helper still keeps the workspace lint gate red for the `ffs-cli` test target.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`

## Expected fix direction
Factor the helper into smaller leaf/item/payload helpers or add targeted structure so the test harness stays under the project’s pedantic clippy budget without suppressing the lint globally.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:03:53.691639847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:03:53.691639847Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clippy","ffs-cli","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}

- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` moves past the current `ffs-core` request-scope failures
- `ffs-core` trait and impl signatures are consistent
- error mapping uses a valid `FfsError` variant or helper

+{"id":"bd-ymg8v","title":"[HIGH] build_btrfs_readdir_image fixture leaves are missing tree_block_csum stamping","description":"# Review finding

`build_btrfs_readdir_image()` builds synthetic root-tree and fs-tree leaves for the readdir suite, but it never stamps `tree_block_csum` on either leaf.

## Evidence
- Builder: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23165-23311`
- It writes the root-tree leaf at `root_tree_logical = 0x4000` and the fs-tree leaf at `fs_tree_logical = 0x20000`.
- Unlike the fsops fixtures, this builder never invokes the existing checksum helper (`stamp_btrfs_test_tree_block_crc32c`), and it switches to `nodesize = 16384`, so both synthetic tree blocks must be stamped consistently with the parser’s current checksum expectations.
- The workspace rerun still fails all readdir-specific consumers with `Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`:
  - `walk_btrfs_dir_entry_items_includes_dir_index_items`
  - `btrfs_readdir_mixed_types`
  - `btrfs_readdir_empty_directory_returns_dot_dotdot`
  - `btrfs_readdir_sorted_by_index`
  - `btrfs_readdir_offset_pagination`
  - `btrfs_readdir_special_characters_in_names`

## Why this is high severity
This is a second stale synthetic-fixture family independent of the minimal open/root-tree fixture. It blocks all of the new btrfs directory-iteration regression coverage.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

## Expected fix direction
Stamp checksums for both generated leaves after all items/payloads are finalized, with nodesize-aware coverage for the 16 KiB fs-tree case.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:05:03.818965727Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:05:03.818965727Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","fixtures","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}

Now 5 tests run by default (read_hello_txt, readdir_root, read_nested_file, getattr_file_metadata, readlink_and_symlink_detection). Write-path and btrfs tests remain gated with #[ignore]. All gates pass (check, clippy, fmt).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
